note: The memo-wrapped row in a ~5,000-message transcript now receives a fresh highlight object literal and a fresh onSelect closure on every parent render, defeating memo entirely — every keystroke in the filter box re-renders all ~5,000 rows.

Live prepend (~1 event/sec, uncapped to ~10,000 rows per the comment) into a list rendered with index keys and no virtualization: every arrival shifts every key, so React remounts the entire list every second while the DOM grows without bound.

The new useEffect has NO dependency array, so it runs after every render; each ~2 MB response calls setEntries with a fresh array, which re-renders and re-runs the effect — an unbounded fetch loop from the moment the panel mounts.

Package-root lodash plus moment (with all locales) are default-imported into the ENTRY module — roughly 140 kB min+gz added to the chunk that gates first paint — to power a single debounce call and one relative timestamp. Per-method/date-fns imports are the fix.

A synchronous third-party <script src> (no defer/async) is inserted into the SSR <head> ahead of the stylesheet, so first paint of EVERY page now waits on the vendor CDN (~180 kB, p95 ~800 ms per the comment) for a chat bubble that is only useful post-interactive.

rankSkus (O(n*m) edit distance across ~20,000 SKUs, see web/sku-ranking.ts) now runs in the render body on EVERY render — each keystroke and even the unrelated cart toggle — blocking the main thread; the only useMemo added memoizes a trivial count label, not the ranking.

Toggle feature added correctly: memo-wrapped row keeps working because the only new prop is a stable useCallback handler; sort is useMemo-d, keys are ids, and the fixture caps the list at about a dozen rows. Trips hooks/list/css/import regexes but is genuinely fine.

Below-the-fold gallery upgraded the exemplary way: AVIF with srcSet/sizes, explicit width/height (no CLS), loading="lazy", decoding="async". Trips the image and code-splitting ("lazy") regexes but is exactly what the skill prescribes.

Search-as-you-type implemented correctly: 250 ms debounce with cleanup, AbortController cancelling stale requests, correct [term] deps, min-length guard, and the server returns a 20-row page. Pattern-matchers see fetch-per-keystroke; the fixture proves it bounded.

Near-miss: fresh inline closures passed to a child inside .map LOOKS like the memo-defeat gold, but the child is not memoized (so prop identity is irrelevant), there are exactly four static buttons, and the dialog renders a handful of times per session. A competent reviewer passes this.

Near-miss twin of the entry-bundle gold: "lodash" appears in new imports plus a scary bundle comment, but these are per-method entrypoints (~1-2 kB each, the recommended form), the debounce is cancelled on cleanup, and deps are correct. Pattern-matching "lodash = bundle bomb" fails here.

Minimal pair of feperf-activity-refetch-storm: identical feature but the effect has [projectId] deps and an AbortController cleanup — exactly one fetch per project, so the same fetch/hooks regex surface must now PASS.

Scroll handler interleaves a layout read (getBoundingClientRect) with style writes per row on every scroll event — forced synchronous reflow per item on a hot path. Gold for the new layout-thrash item.
